label: Uncomplicated pityriasis rosea without systemic symptoms
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated pityriasis rosea.
No severe cutaneous adverse reaction, mucosal lesions, infection, systemic illness, pregnancy, immunocompromised host, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Severe cutaneous adverse reaction, mucosal lesions, skin peeling/sloughing, target lesions, cellulitis, abscess, fever, or systemic illness.
Pregnancy, medication reaction concern, immunocompromised host, pediatric pathway, STI/syphilis concern, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if symptoms persist, worsen, recur, or the diagnosis is uncertain.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports pityriasis-rosea framing, typical herald-patch/scaly-rash course, itching, and resolution over weeks. Autonomous Phase 1751-1800 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated pityriasis rosea without drug-rash warning signs, mucosal involvement, infection, pregnancy, STI mimic concern, systemic illness, immunocompromise, pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports pityriasis-rosea framing, typical herald-patch/scaly-rash course, itching, and resolution over weeks. Autonomous Phase 1751-1800 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated pityriasis rosea without drug-rash warning signs, mucosal involvement, infection, pregnancy, STI mimic concern, systemic illness, immunocompromise, pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated pityriasis rosea
no mucosal lesions, skin peeling, medication reaction, fever, or systemic illness documented
no pregnancy, STI, immunocompromised, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
